title: blazewrit harness — Test step structured_output (RED tests + coverage/profile/migration-validate)
description: OUTPUT schema for the Test step. Test writes FAILING (RED) tests that verify Spec ACs; in Bug Fix it reproduces; in Performance it profiles/measures; in Migration it validates. TARGET RUNTIME: grammar-constrained generation (M1 = SHAPE; the model literally cannot emit a wrong shape/enum/missing-required field). Truth-bearing fields (RED-confirmed counts, profile baselines, migration dry-run, AC traceability, declared next step) are closed by SIBLING M2 validator_contracts listed in x-validator-contract annotations and in the step manifest — grammar enforces SHAPE only. INPUT CONTRACT: exactly one of (Spec | Decide) — modeled as the `input` meta field, a oneOf discriminated by `input_kind` so grammar enforces exactly-one-of. §5: every cross-step ref (based_on, bug_ref) is a Postgres RowRef, never a file path. R23: every measured count is a CountClaim. M3: every tool-dependent fact (RED confirmation, firebat scan, perf baseline, migration dry-run) is a Measured|Omitted discriminated union. R13: `unverified` is a KEEP-polarity floor flag (the historical (now-removed) 'No [UNVERIFIED] remain' rule is DELETED — Verify is the single gate).
$comment: ROOT REQUIRED note: tests_written_count/red_confirmed_count ARE root-required but are Measured(CountClaim)|Omitted DEGRADABLE unions (NOT bare CountClaim). The discriminant is WHETHER A BEHAVIOR-TEST RUNNER WAS INVOKED, not whether a `result` is set. (1) Measured branch — emitted whenever the behavior-test runner actually ran over a (possibly partial) suite: the result∈{red,unexpected_green} success paths AND the cap_exceeded escalate path (caps blew mid-activity AFTER partial tests_added were written AND executed to derive per-test RED status, so a real CountClaim exists over the partial suite — counted from that runner's stdout). (2) Omitted branch — emitted on every path where NO behavior-test runner was invoked, regardless of whether a result is set: the no_op / measured (Performance|Migration) paths (reason=not_applicable), the migration-failed|partial escalate (a Migration flow with no behavior runner — the dry-run tool itself ran, the migration script is what broke; reason=not_applicable, decoupled from 'has a result'), and the precondition-or-runner-absent escalate where test code/execution never happened (reason=tool_absent|tool_failed|timeout). See prose 'Count provenance' note.

allOf.$comment: ESCALATE EXCLUSIVITY (prose L74: 'escalate는 result enum 값이 아니다 ... 성공 result 분기와 배타적이다'). escalate is a first-class TERMINAL branch mutually exclusive with the SUCCESS result verdict. When `escalate` is ABSENT, `result` is required (the success/no_op verdict path); when `escalate` is PRESENT, the precondition/in-scope-failure escalation routes purely on `escalate` and `result` is NOT required (it may be omitted entirely — there is no success result and no success output block). TWO PERMITTED CO-OCCURRENCES (escalate accompanying a diagnostic/partial block — neither is a SUCCESS block, so neither violates exclusivity): (1) prose Migration failed|partial path — a migration_validation block whose dry_run_result.value.outcome=failed|partial MAY (and per the M2 contract MUST) co-occur with escalate(failure_origin=test); that measured-but-failed block is where dry_run_detail (the diagnostic) lives. (2) prose Caps mid-activity path (L192-193) — escalate(failure_origin=cap_exceeded) MAY (and per the M2 contract MUST) co-occur with a PARTIAL tests_added (the tests written before caps blew) plus escalate.remaining_targets (the remaining-AC list); the partial tests_added is NOT a success result block (no `result` is set on the escalate path, so it does not derive result=red) — it is partial-progress diagnostic carried alongside the cap escalation. Because the success-block obligations are SUPPRESSED (not forbidden) under escalate, both diagnostic co-occurrences are grammatically permitted; the M2 cap_exceeded contract (see escalate.remaining_targets) requires the partial tests_added + remaining_targets pairing. All OTHER success blocks remain suppressed under escalate.

P1: behavior-test flows must carry a non-empty primary behavior-test block. When result ∈ {red, unexpected_green} AND escalate is absent, at least one of tests_added / coverage_added must be present and non-empty (derived per-test status drives result). measured/no_op flows fill other blocks instead. Guarded by absence of escalate: on escalate these primary-block obligations are SUPPRESSED (escalate is exclusive with the success result blocks).
result=measured (Performance/Migration) requires the measuring primary block — suppressed on escalate.
result=no_op requires no_op_details (legitimately-zero output verdict, not escalate) — suppressed on escalate.

MANDATORY FIREBAT SCAN (prose Activity 7 L46 + agents/test.md L22): a test-writing flow MUST run a firebat scan AFTER writing test code, recording its findings_count or — on absence/fail/timeout — firebat_scan.status=omitted. The trigger is 'test code was written', i.e. a non-empty primary behavior-test block (tests_added / coverage_added) — REGARDLESS of escalate. The escalate-absence guard is DELIBERATELY ABSENT here (Defect-1 fix): the cap_exceeded escalate path writes+executes a PARTIAL tests_added before caps blow (see escalate $comment + tests_written_count Measured branch), so that real test code MUST also carry the mandatory scan — exempting it would let a flow that wrote and ran real test code skip the scan Activity 7 makes obligatory. Precondition/runner-absent escalates carry NO tests_added/coverage_added, so they never trip this. measured/migration/no_op flows that write no test code also do not trip it.

result.$comment: P1+P-residual: the whole result-verdict field, DERIVED from the flow's primary output block (tests_added / coverage_added / profile / migration_validation — NOT tests_added alone). This is the deliberate Investigate-pattern backport (success branches, not just failures). In-scope FAILURES (preconditions, runner-absent, migration dry_run failed|partial, caps) are NOT result values — they route through the separate `escalate` branch with failure_origin.
result.x-validator-contract: M2 (result derivation precedence + measured primary-block selection, prose L72 tie-break + 'measured primary-block 선택' note): (1) BEHAVIOR-TEST PRECEDENCE — if tests_added/coverage_added has any status=RED ⇒ result=red; else if any behavior-test/coverage item is present (all unexpected_green) ⇒ result=unexpected_green; behavior-test/coverage presence OUTRANKS measured (a flow that also carries a measurement does NOT collapse to measured while behavior tests exist). (2) MEASURED PRIMARY SELECTION — result=measured derives from exactly ONE primary measuring block, the flow's actual primary per orchestrator chain (Performance⇒profile, Migration⇒migration_validation); the non-primary measuring block (if any) is enrichment metadata. (3) NO RUBBER-STAMP — result=measured is FORBIDDEN when the chosen primary's measurement is omitted/failed (profile.baseline.status=omitted, or migration_validation.dry_run_result.status=omitted, or dry_run_result.value.outcome∈{failed,partial}); those route to escalate(failure_origin=test) with result omitted (prose L182-184). Grammar accepts these combinations; this contract is the named rejection hook.
result.description: Test result verdict. 'red' = at least one written tests_added/coverage_added item has status=RED (→ Test⇄Implement loop). 'unexpected_green' = all filled behavior-test/coverage items are status=unexpected_green (→ signal Verify, not escalate); behavior-test/coverage presence outranks measured (see x-validator-contract). 'measured' = the flow's PRIMARY measuring block measured cleanly — Performance profile baseline=measured OR Migration dry_run outcome=ok (→ signal Verify); forbidden when the chosen primary's measurement is omitted/failed (routes to escalate). 'no_op' = legitimately-zero output (well-formed empty AC / Decide-driven 0-derivation) carrying no_op_details. Migration dry_run failed|partial and other in-scope failures do NOT map here — they escalate (failure_origin=test).

no_op_details.$comment: Required when result=no_op (enforced by root allOf). Reuses the Investigate no_op_details shape. A verdict (legitimately-zero output), NOT an escalate.
no_op_details.description: Details for result=no_op (legitimately-zero output). reason = why there is justly nothing to write/measure; evidence = the upstream fact ref grounding the empty-by-design conclusion; suggested_action = abandon | reframe_request.

evidence.description: Upstream fact ref grounding the justly-empty conclusion.

escalate.$comment: Mechanical-error / in-scope-failure terminal carrier — present INSTEAD of a result verdict path's success blocks (preconditions PC, test-runner absent, migration dry_run failed|partial, caps mid-activity). failure_origin reuses _defs FailureOrigin restricted to the Test-reachable origins {spec, decide, test, cap_exceeded}. NOT request_upstream_deepen (Decide-only, principle 2). EXCLUSIVE with success: when `escalate` is present (a) `result` is NOT required (root allOf only requires `result` when escalate is absent) and (b) the three conditional primary-block requirements (tests_added/coverage_added for red|unexpected_green, profile|migration_validation for measured, no_op_details for no_op) are SUPPRESSED — the orchestrator routes purely on `escalate`. CAP_EXCEEDED PARTIAL CO-OCCURRENCE (prose L192-193): on failure_origin=cap_exceeded the step ALSO emits the partial tests_added (tests written before caps blew) and escalate.remaining_targets (the remaining-AC list); x-validator-contract (M2): failure_origin=cap_exceeded ⇒ remaining_targets present (the unaddressed ACs) and the partial tests_added carried alongside — this partial block is diagnostic, not a result-deriving success block (no `result` is set).
escalate.description: OPTIONAL escalate carrier for Test's in-scope failures (mechanical errors): missing/malformed upstream (failure_origin=spec|decide), test-runner absent or migration dry_run failed|partial (failure_origin=test), or caps blown mid-activity (failure_origin=cap_exceeded). reason + evidence (ref or file:line) diagnose it. Routed out-of-band by the orchestrator; never via request_upstream_deepen.

failure_origin.description: Which step the in-scope failure is attributed to. Test-reachable subset of _defs FailureOrigin: spec/decide (missing-or-malformed upstream), test (runner absent / migration dry_run failed|partial), cap_exceeded (caps blown). The shared FailureOrigin $def is reused (M4 single-source) but restricted by an enum allOf to the Test-reachable values only.

reason.description: Human-readable diagnosis of the mechanical error.

evidence.description: Evidence for the failure (a RowRef-style ref or a file:line pointer).

remaining_targets.$comment: PARTIAL-PROGRESS carrier for the cap_exceeded path (prose 'Caps mid-activity hit' L192-193): when caps blow mid-activity the step fills tests_added with the tests written so far AND records the still-unaddressed ACs/targets here. This is the home for the 'remaining-AC list' the prose mandates; absent on non-cap escalations.
remaining_targets.description: REMAINING AC / target list for the cap_exceeded partial-output path. When Step Depth Policy caps blow before every AC got a test, the step emits the partial tests_added AND lists the unaddressed ACs/derivation targets here (each a within-Spec AC id or a Decide-derived target). Present only on failure_origin=cap_exceeded; the orchestrator uses it to resume/route. Absent on spec/decide/test escalations.

input.$comment: Input contract: exactly one of (Spec | Decide). Modeled as a oneOf discriminated by input_kind so grammar enforces exactly-one-of (R1/M1). The actual upstream rows are RowRefs in based_on; this meta records WHICH single input drove the step and its row.
input.description: INPUT CONTRACT (required-one-of). Exactly ONE upstream input drives Test: a Spec output (flows that have a Spec stage — Feature etc.) OR a Decide output (Spec-less chains — Bug Fix reproduce, Bug Fix Unreproducible hypothesis, Migration validate, Test-flow Decide(Plan)). Grammar enforces exactly one branch via the input_kind discriminant; per-branch `required` forces the matching row ref. x-validator-contract (M2): input_kind branch MUST match the orchestrator's chain (reviewer verifies flow chain).

spec_ref.description: RowRef to the driving Spec step_runs row (acceptance_criteria / tasks / code_architecture).

decide_ref.description: RowRef to the driving Decide step_runs row for Spec-less chains. The real Decide fields read are mode-dependent: mode=record → decision/rationale; mode=plan → options_considered/chosen; mode=design → chosen_architecture/requirements/policies (the legacy `option_selection`/`decision_record` field names do NOT exist in the Decide schema — P3/P8 correction).

tests_added.$comment: Core output for behavior-test flows. tests_added[{file_path, test_name, ac_ref?, status:RED|unexpected_green, unexpected_green_reason?}]. status is DERIVED from actual execution: RED = ran and failed; unexpected_green = ran and passed (a first-class normal outcome, NOT escalate). minItems removed and conditionally required only when result ∈ {red, unexpected_green} (see top-level allOf) — measured/no_op/coverage-only flows fill other blocks. Each item carries optional RED-confirmation evidence (M3 degradable), a provenance source_tool (R26 floor), and the R13 unverified KEEP-polarity flag.
tests_added.x-validator-contract: M2 (source_tool provenance consistency, Defect-3 hole closure): each tests_added item carries TWO runner-provenance fields that grammar cannot couple — the item-level source_tool (R26 floor) and, on the Measured branch of red_confirmation, the inherited Measured.source_tool. These name the SAME run (the item-level source_tool IS the runner that produced red_confirmation's RED/GREEN evidence), so when red_confirmation.status=measured the validator asserts tests_added[i].source_tool == red_confirmation.source_tool (the Measured branch's inherited source_tool — same runner identity); a producer naming 'bun' at item level and 'firebat' inside red_confirmation is REJECTED as inconsistent provenance. On red_confirmation.status=omitted there is no measured source_tool to cross-check (only the item-level floor applies).
tests_added.description: The behavior tests this step wrote. Each entry is a single test traced to the AC (or reproduction/coverage target) it verifies. status is DERIVED from the actual test run: 'RED' = executed and observed failing (Implement turns GREEN); 'unexpected_green' = executed and observed passing (RED was intended but it passed — a normal outcome: already_satisfied / not_reproducible / coverage_over_existing / p0_retroactive). ac_ref (OPTIONAL) is the within-Spec AC identifier (e.g. 'AC-001') for Spec-driven tests, or the reproduction/coverage/derivation target for Decide-driven tests; unexpected_green/no_op cases may legitimately lack an AC id. M2 (test-reviewer/AC traceability): every Spec AC has at least one tests_added entry whose ac_ref names it; reverse, every ac_ref resolves to a real AC in the spec_ref row. M2 (source_tool consistency): the item-level source_tool and the red_confirmation Measured-branch source_tool name the SAME runner (see x-validator-contract).

allOf.$comment: ANTI-SELF-ASSERTION FLOOR (prose L50/L100 + Defect-3 hole closure): status is DERIVED from actual execution, and M2 re-executes/re-hashes the recorded outcome — so the execution-evidence carrier red_confirmation MUST be present for any status∈{RED,unexpected_green} (i.e. always, given the status enum). Without it the M2 re-execution/re-hash contract has no command/sha to anchor to and status collapses to the exact self-assertion the design forbids. The Measured|Omitted union still lets a degraded run surface as the Omitted branch (R22 first-class absence) — but the FIELD itself is no longer omissible.

file_path.description: Path to the test file written (a real on-disk READ/WRITE path — code under git, NOT a §5 workflow-artifact RowRef; tests are versioned source files per DECISIONS §5 'code + spec/ADR = file (git)').

test_name.description: The test case identifier (function/describe-it name) that fails RED.

ac_ref.description: OPTIONAL. The acceptance-criterion identifier this test verifies (Spec-internal id, e.g. 'AC-001'). For non-Spec flows (reproduce/coverage) this names the reproduction or coverage target the test traces to. unexpected_green/no_op cases may legitimately lack an AC id. M2 (R-traceability): when present must resolve to a real AC/target in the input row.

bug_ref.description: OPTIONAL JOIN KEY for the reproduction↔tests_added pairing (Bug Fix flow). When this tests_added entry is the reproduce test paired with a reproduction block, bug_ref carries the SAME §5 RowRef as that reproduction.bug_ref — this is the joinable field the M2 pairing contract matches on (reproduced=true ⇒ this entry status=RED; reproduced=false ⇒ status=unexpected_green/not_reproducible). Absent on non-reproduce tests. NEVER a file path.

status.description: Per-test run polarity, DERIVED from actual execution (not self-asserted). 'RED' = ran and failed (Implement turns GREEN); 'unexpected_green' = ran and passed (RED intended but it passed — a first-class normal outcome, signals Verify, not escalate). When 'unexpected_green', unexpected_green_reason is required.

unexpected_green_reason.$comment: Required-when status=unexpected_green (enforced by the item-level if/then below). Enumerates the legitimate reasons a RED-intended test passes.
unexpected_green_reason.description: Why this test came up GREEN despite RED intent. Required when status=unexpected_green. 'already_satisfied' = AC already met; 'not_reproducible' = bug did not reproduce (already fixed); 'coverage_over_existing' = coverage test over already-GREEN code; 'p0_retroactive' = Bug Fix P0 retroactive test written after the emergency fix merged GREEN.

red_confirmation.$comment: M3 DEGRADE — confirming the run polarity requires RUNNING the test; that run can fail/timeout/be unavailable (firebat absent). Absence-of-VALUE is a first-class branch (Measured|Omitted), NOT a null or placeholder. Measured.value carries the observed outcome. REQUIRED per-item (item-level allOf if/then above): because status is derived from execution and M2 re-executes/re-hashes it, the evidence carrier cannot be field-omitted for any status — a non-runnable case surfaces as the Omitted branch, NOT a missing field.
red_confirmation.description: Evidence from actually running this test (the observed run polarity) — the per-test re-hashable anchor M2 re-executes against. REQUIRED for every item (status is always RED|unexpected_green). M3 Measured|Omitted discriminated union on `status`: 'measured' = the test was executed and its outcome observed (value.failed=true ⇒ RED; value.failed=false ⇒ unexpected_green); 'omitted' = the run could not be performed (e.g. test runner/firebat tool_failed/timeout/unavailable — R22 first-class absence). x-validator-contract (M2/R20-adjacent): orchestrator re-executes the test and asserts the recorded outcome (a self-asserted polarity is not truth).

failed.description: Observed run polarity: true = the test FAILED (RED); false = the test PASSED (unexpected_green). M2 re-executes and confirms the recorded value matches the item's status (failed=true ⇔ status=RED, failed=false ⇔ status=unexpected_green).

command.description: Exact command run to confirm RED (e.g. 'bun test path/to/file').

raw_stdout_sha256.description: REQUIRED SHA-256 of the captured runner stdout — the load-bearing anchor M2 re-hashes to re-derive the failing/passing outcome (mirrors CountClaim, where the sha is likewise required). Without it the M2 re-hash contract has nothing to re-hash and status collapses to self-assertion.

source_tool.description: PROVENANCE (R26 floor, required). The tool/runner that produced this test entry's RED evidence (e.g. 'bun', 'firebat'). A test entry without a source_tool is unsourced and rejected.

unverified.$comment: R13 floor, KEEP polarity. NO 'no unverified remain' forbid (historical 'No [UNVERIFIED] remain' rule DELETED). Verify is the single gate.
unverified.description: R13 KEEP-polarity floor flag. true = a claim about this test (e.g. it covers the AC's behavior, or a training-data-based assertion) was not verified by Test itself and MUST propagate downstream to Verify. Never forbidden true at this intermediate step.

coverage_added.$comment: Activity 3 (Refactor / Test flows). First-class PRIMARY output for coverage flows — participates in result derivation exactly like tests_added (any item status=RED ⇒ result=red; all unexpected_green ⇒ result=unexpected_green). A coverage-only flow MUST NOT be misclassified as no_op.
coverage_added.description: OPTIONAL — Refactor/Test coverage flows. Coverage tests added over previously-uncovered areas. Coverage over already-GREEN code legitimately yields status=unexpected_green(coverage_over_existing). Same per-item run-polarity semantics as tests_added; folded into result derivation.

allOf.$comment: ANTI-SELF-ASSERTION FLOOR (prose L50/L117 + Defect-1/Defect-2 closure): coverage_added is a first-class PRIMARY result-deriving block exactly like tests_added, so its per-item status (always RED|unexpected_green) is execution-derived and re-hashed by M2 — the execution-evidence carrier red_confirmation MUST be present on every item (mirrors tests_added). Without it the red_confirmed_count count-denominator re-derivation has no per-item raw_stdout_sha256 anchor for the coverage_added portion (Defect-2), and status collapses to the self-assertion the design forbids. The Measured|Omitted union still surfaces a degraded run as the Omitted branch (R22) — the FIELD is not omissible.

file_path.description: Path to the coverage test file written (git-versioned code, not a §5 RowRef).

area.description: The previously-uncovered area this test covers.

status.description: Per-coverage-test run polarity, DERIVED from execution: 'RED' = ran and failed; 'unexpected_green' = ran and passed (coverage over already-GREEN code is the normal coverage_over_existing case).

unexpected_green_reason.$comment: Coverage flows are Refactor/Test (prose L42), NOT Bug Fix — so the Bug-Fix-only reasons not_reproducible (reproduce) and p0_retroactive (P0 retroactive) cannot legitimately arise here. Restricted to the coverage-reachable reasons only (prose L113 pairs coverage with coverage_over_existing).
unexpected_green_reason.description: Why this coverage test came up GREEN. Required when status=unexpected_green (typically 'coverage_over_existing'; 'already_satisfied' when the covered area's behavior was already met). The Bug-Fix-only reasons (not_reproducible / p0_retroactive) are excluded — coverage flows are Refactor/Test, not Bug Fix.

red_confirmation.$comment: M3 DEGRADE — mirrors tests_added.red_confirmation (Defect-1/Defect-2 closure). coverage_added is a first-class PRIMARY result-deriving block, so confirming its per-item run polarity requires RUNNING the coverage test; that run can fail/timeout/be unavailable. Absence-of-VALUE is a first-class branch (Measured|Omitted), NOT a null/placeholder. REQUIRED per-item (item-level allOf if/then above): status is execution-derived (always RED|unexpected_green) and M2 re-executes/re-hashes it, so the evidence carrier cannot be field-omitted — a non-runnable case surfaces as the Omitted branch. This per-item raw_stdout_sha256 is the anchor red_confirmed_count's count-denominator re-derivation needs for the coverage_added portion (Defect-2).
red_confirmation.description: Evidence from actually running this coverage test (the observed run polarity) — the per-test re-hashable anchor M2 re-executes against. REQUIRED for every item (status is always RED|unexpected_green). M3 Measured|Omitted discriminated union on `status`: 'measured' = the test was executed and its outcome observed (value.failed=true ⇒ RED; value.failed=false ⇒ unexpected_green); 'omitted' = the run could not be performed (test runner/firebat tool_failed/timeout/unavailable — R22 first-class absence). x-validator-contract (M2): orchestrator re-executes the test and asserts the recorded outcome; red_confirmed_count re-derives its RED-count denominator from these anchors.

failed.description: Observed run polarity: true = the coverage test FAILED (RED); false = it PASSED (unexpected_green). M2 re-executes and confirms the recorded value matches the item's status (failed=true ⇔ status=RED, failed=false ⇔ status=unexpected_green).

command.description: Exact command run to confirm the coverage test's run polarity (e.g. 'bun test path/to/file').

raw_stdout_sha256.description: REQUIRED SHA-256 of the captured runner stdout — the load-bearing anchor M2 re-hashes to re-derive the failing/passing outcome and the red_confirmed_count RED-count denominator (mirrors tests_added.red_confirmation). Without it the M2 re-hash contract has nothing to re-hash and status collapses to self-assertion.

source_tool.description: PROVENANCE (R26 floor, required) — mirrors tests_added. The tool/runner that produced this coverage entry's run evidence (e.g. 'bun', 'firebat'). A coverage entry without a source_tool is unsourced and rejected. coverage_added is a first-class PRIMARY result-deriving block, equivalent to tests_added, so it carries the same irreducible floor. M2 (source_tool consistency, mirrors tests_added): when red_confirmation.status=measured the item-level source_tool == red_confirmation.source_tool (same runner identity).

unverified.$comment: R13 floor, KEEP polarity — mirrors tests_added. coverage_added is a first-class PRIMARY block, so its unverified claim must have a carrier to propagate to Verify.
unverified.description: R13 KEEP-polarity floor flag (required). true = a claim about this coverage test was not verified by Test itself and MUST propagate downstream to Verify. Never forbidden true at this intermediate step.

tests_written_count.$comment: R23 count TYPE wrapped in an M3 DEGRADABLE union (Measured(CountClaim)|Omitted). 'N tests written' is a measured count NEVER a bare integer. The Measured|Omitted discriminant is WHETHER A BEHAVIOR-TEST RUNNER WAS INVOKED, not whether a `result` is set: the result∈{red,unexpected_green} paths AND the cap_exceeded escalate path (the runner ran over the PARTIAL suite, so a real CountClaim exists) emit Measured; every path with no behavior runner (no_op / measured / migration-failed|partial escalate / precondition-or-runner-absent escalate) emits Omitted (R22 first-class absence). Measured branch's value is the R23 CountClaim (command + sha256 of stdout).
tests_written_count.x-validator-contract: M2 (Omitted.reason ↔ runner-invocation coupling, prose 'Count provenance'): grammar leaves the shared Omitted.reason enum broad (tool_absent|tool_failed|timeout|unavailable|skipped|not_applicable), so this code check binds tests_written_count/red_confirmed_count's branch+reason to WHETHER A BEHAVIOR-TEST RUNNER WAS INVOKED (NOT to whether a result is set). (A) RUNNER RAN ⇒ Measured: result∈{red,unexpected_green} AND failure_origin=cap_exceeded (the runner ran over the partial tests_added before caps blew, so a real CountClaim over the partial suite exists). (B) NO RUNNER ⇒ Omitted: result∈{no_op, measured} ⇒ Omitted.reason MUST be not_applicable (no runner invoked, not a tool degrade); failure_origin=test caused by a Migration dry_run outcome∈{failed,partial} (a Migration flow — no behavior runner ran; the dry-run tool itself ran fine, the migration script broke) ⇒ Omitted.reason MUST be not_applicable (decoupled from 'has a result' — bound to 'no behavior-test runner was invoked'); a precondition-or-runner-ABSENT escalate (no test code/execution) ⇒ Omitted.reason ∈ {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout}. The values unavailable|skipped are NOT valid for these two counts; a producer emitting result=no_op with reason=skipped passes grammar but this contract rejects it. COUNT-DENOMINATOR (Defect-5 fix): on the Measured branch re-parse source.raw_stdout and assert tests_written_count.value == the TOTAL number of written behavior-test items (tests_added + coverage_added items, all statuses; on cap_exceeded this is the partial suite). Do NOT equate it to red_confirmed_count when unexpected_green items exist.
tests_written_count.description: R23 CountClaim wrapped in M3 Measured|Omitted. The branch is chosen by whether a behavior-test runner ran, NOT by whether a result is set. 'measured' = a behavior-test runner ran and the written-test count was counted from its stdout (value = CountClaim: command + raw_stdout_sha256) — the result∈{red,unexpected_green} paths AND the cap_exceeded escalate path (runner ran over the partial suite). 'omitted' = no behavior runner was invoked: no_op legitimately-zero / Performance|Migration measured path / migration-failed|partial escalate (Migration flow, no behavior runner) ⇒ reason=not_applicable; precondition-or-runner-absent escalate (no test code/execution) ⇒ tool_absent|tool_failed|timeout. The broader unavailable|skipped enum values are NOT valid here — see x-validator-contract. Bare integer forbidden. M2: on the measured branch re-parse source.raw_stdout and assert value == the TOTAL written behavior-test count (tests_added + coverage_added items, all statuses) — NOT only the RED ones.

red_confirmed_count.$comment: R23 count TYPE wrapped in an M3 DEGRADABLE union (Measured(CountClaim)|Omitted). 'N RED confirmed' is a measured count from re-running the tests. The Measured|Omitted discriminant is WHETHER A BEHAVIOR-TEST RUNNER WAS INVOKED, not whether a result is set: the result∈{red,unexpected_green} paths AND the cap_exceeded escalate path (the runner ran over the partial suite, deriving per-test RED status) emit Measured; every path with no behavior runner (no_op / measured / migration-failed|partial escalate / precondition-or-runner-absent escalate) emits Omitted.
red_confirmed_count.x-validator-contract: M2 (Omitted.reason ↔ runner-invocation coupling, prose 'Count provenance'): same binding as tests_written_count, keyed on WHETHER A BEHAVIOR-TEST RUNNER WAS INVOKED (NOT on whether a result is set). (A) RUNNER RAN ⇒ Measured: result∈{red,unexpected_green} AND failure_origin=cap_exceeded (runner ran over the partial tests_added, so the RED-confirmed count exists over the partial suite). (B) NO RUNNER ⇒ Omitted: result∈{no_op, measured} ⇒ Omitted.reason MUST be not_applicable; a migration dry_run outcome∈{failed,partial} escalate (Migration flow, no behavior runner; dry-run tool ran fine, migration script broke) ⇒ Omitted.reason MUST be not_applicable (decoupled from 'has a result' — bound to 'no behavior-test runner was invoked'); a precondition-or-runner-ABSENT escalate ⇒ Omitted.reason ∈ {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout}; the shared-enum values unavailable|skipped are rejected for this count. COUNT-DENOMINATOR (Defect-5 fix): on the Measured branch re-execute the suite, re-derive the failing count from raw_stdout, and assert red_confirmed_count.value == the number of tests_added/coverage_added items with status=RED (the RED-confirmed subset, re-derived from runner stdout; on cap_exceeded over the partial suite) — NOT tests_added.length and NOT tests_written_count.value, since a mixed red/unexpected_green flow (prose L72) has fewer RED items than total written items.
red_confirmed_count.description: R23 CountClaim wrapped in M3 Measured|Omitted. The branch is chosen by whether a behavior-test runner ran, NOT by whether a result is set. 'measured' = the suite was RUN and the RED-confirmed count derived from its stdout (value = CountClaim: command + raw_stdout_sha256) — the result∈{red,unexpected_green} paths AND the cap_exceeded escalate path (runner ran over the partial suite). 'omitted' = no behavior suite was run: no_op / Performance|Migration measured / migration-failed|partial escalate (Migration flow, no behavior runner) ⇒ reason=not_applicable; precondition-or-runner-absent escalate ⇒ tool_absent|tool_failed|timeout. unavailable|skipped NOT valid — see x-validator-contract. Self-asserted 'N RED' is the exact R23 hallucination this forbids. M2: on the measured branch re-execute the suite, re-derive the failing count, and assert value == the count of tests_added/coverage_added items with status=RED — NOT the total written count (which tests_written_count carries) when statuses are mixed.

reproduction.$comment: Bug Fix flow only. {test_path, bug_ref, reproduced}. bug_ref is a §5 RowRef (the Decide/bug-source row), NOT a file path. reproduction is METADATA, NOT a primary result-deriving block — it never appears in the root allOf primary-block anyOf. Every reproduction (reproduced true AND false) is always carried by a PAIRED tests_added entry (the reproduce test) whose status drives result derivation; reproduction itself contributes no result. See x-validator-contract.
reproduction.x-validator-contract: M2 (reproduction↔tests_added pairing): every reproduction MUST be mirrored by a paired tests_added entry, JOINED ON bug_ref — the paired entry is the tests_added item whose bug_ref equals this reproduction.bug_ref (the joinable key; with multiple bugs the validator matches reproduction.bug_ref ⇔ tests_added[].bug_ref). For the matched entry: reproduced=true ⇒ status=RED; reproduced=false ⇒ status=unexpected_green with unexpected_green_reason=not_reproducible. result is DERIVED from the paired tests_added entry's status, NOT from reproduction (reproduction is metadata, not a primary result block). Grammar cannot express this cross-field pairing/join; it is enforced as an M2 contract. JOIN COMPLETENESS (Defect-5 fix — bug_ref is OPTIONAL on tests_added, so the join key can be missing on one side): when a reproduction block is present, EXACTLY ONE tests_added entry MUST carry a matching bug_ref; a reproduction whose bug_ref joins to ZERO tests_added entries (no paired reproduce test — e.g. a stale/malformed upstream that emitted reproduced=true with no tests_added carrying that bug_ref) OR to MORE THAN ONE entry (ambiguous join) is REJECTED. REPRODUCTION-ABSENCE (Defect-2 hole closure): reproduction MUST be ABSENT whenever there is no joinable tests_added entry to pair with — i.e. on result=no_op (zero written tests), result=measured (Performance/Migration — no behavior test), and escalate-only paths (no test code). A reproduction co-occurring with any of these (no joinable tests_added[].bug_ref) is REJECTED.
reproduction.description: OPTIONAL — Bug Fix flow. The test that reproduces the bug (RED; Implement's fix turns it GREEN). Present only on Bug Fix chains. test_path is a real on-disk test file path (git-versioned code); bug_ref is a §5 RowRef to the upstream Decide or Investigate step_runs row describing the bug (the legacy `decision_record` field name does NOT exist in the Decide schema — P3/P8 correction). reproduction is METADATA, NOT a result-deriving primary block: every reproduction (true and false) is paired with a tests_added entry that drives result derivation. M2 (test-reviewer): the reproduction test actually reproduces the bug, and the reproduction↔tests_added pairing holds (see x-validator-contract).

test_path.description: On-disk path of the reproduction test file (git-versioned code, not a workflow RowRef).

bug_ref.description: §5 RowRef to the upstream row that defines the bug (the Decide or Investigate step_runs row describing the bug). NEVER a file path. JOIN KEY: the paired reproduce test in tests_added carries the SAME bug_ref so the M2 pairing contract can match reproduction↔tests_added (see x-validator-contract).

reproduced.description: Whether the reproduction test actually reproduced the bug. true = bug reproduced (RED). false = bug NOT reproduced (already fixed) → the corresponding tests_added entry has status=unexpected_green(not_reproducible).

profile.$comment: Performance flow only. {metric, baseline, target}. baseline is MEASURED (CountClaim) when available, but profiling tools are degradable → baseline is a Measured(CountClaim)|Omitted union (R22/M3). target is the design goal, not a measurement. ANTI-RUBBER-STAMP (mirrors migration_validation L369): grammar accepts result=measured alongside baseline.status=omitted; the inner measured-state coupling is closed by x-validator-contract, not grammar.
profile.x-validator-contract: M2 (result=measured ⇒ profile.baseline.status=measured, prose L69/L116/L200): when result=measured is derived from this Performance block, profile.baseline.status MUST be measured (a measured profile result cannot rest on an omitted baseline). Conversely when profile.baseline.status=omitted: if the profiler is the flow's SOLE output the step escalates (failure_origin=test, prose L174) and does NOT set result=measured; if behavior tests also exist they drive result (red|unexpected_green) and profile is enrichment metadata. Grammar accepts result=measured with baseline.status=omitted; this contract rejects that rubber-stamp.
profile.description: OPTIONAL — Performance flow. The profiling measurement: which metric, its measured baseline, and the target. Present only on Performance chains. baseline is M3-degradable (perf tooling can be ABSENT/FAIL/timeout — R22 first-class absence), and when measured it is a CountClaim (a measured number, not a bare integer — R23). target is the goal the benchmark asserts against (an M2/R20 verify_probe-style threshold, not itself a measurement). x-validator-contract (M2): result=measured ⇒ baseline.status=measured; baseline.status=omitted routes to escalate (sole output) or to the behavior-test result (non-sole) — never to result=measured.

unit.description: REQUIRED measurement unit for `metric` and the baseline/target values (e.g. 'ms', 'MB', 'ops/s').

baseline.$comment: M3 + R23. Measured branch carries a CountClaim as its value (measured number with command+sha256); Omitted branch when the perf tool is absent/failed/timeout.
baseline.description: The measured baseline for `metric`. M3 Measured|Omitted on `status`: 'measured' = baseline obtained, value is an R23 CountClaim (the measured number + the command/raw_stdout that produced it); 'omitted' = perf tool absent/failed/timeout (R22 first-class absence). NOT nullable, NOT a placeholder. x-validator-contract (M2): re-parse the CountClaim source and re-derive the baseline number.

target.$comment: R20 — a target is a re-runnable assertion (command + expected_result), not a measurement. The `comparator` sibling encodes the baseline→target direction the expected_result is checked against.
target.description: The performance target the benchmark must eventually meet, expressed as an R20 VerifyProbe (command + expected_result). SHAPE only; M2 re-executes the probe against the implementation (Verify), not Test. Test writes the RED benchmark; it does NOT pass yet. The comparator field gives the threshold direction (baseline→target).

comparator.description: Threshold direction for baseline→target (e.g. p95 latency lte target). One of lte|gte|lt|gt|eq.

migration_validation.$comment: Migration flow only. {script_path, dry_run_result}. dry-run executes the migration tool → degradable (Measured|Omitted, R22/M3). script_path is git-versioned code, not a RowRef.
migration_validation.description: OPTIONAL — Migration flow. Validation of the migration script (reversibility / data-integrity / rollback). Present only on Migration chains. script_path is the on-disk migration script (git-versioned). dry_run_result is M3-degradable: running a dry-run can fail/timeout or the tool can be absent — absence is a first-class Omitted branch, not a null/placeholder. x-validator-contract (M2): re-execute the dry-run and confirm the recorded outcome.

script_path.description: On-disk path of the migration script validated (git-versioned code, not a workflow RowRef).

dry_run_result.$comment: M3 DEGRADE — the dry-run outcome. Measured branch carries the executed result + provenance; Omitted when the dry-run could not run. ANTI-RUBBER-STAMP (prose L76): outcome failed|partial is an in-scope Test failure, NOT a measured success — grammar cannot couple it to escalate, so see x-validator-contract.
dry_run_result.x-validator-contract: M2 (anti-rubber-stamp, prose L76/L130/L200): when dry_run_result.value.outcome ∈ {failed, partial}, the output MUST carry escalate with failure_origin=test AND MUST NOT set result=measured (result is omitted on the escalate path); dry_run_detail MUST be present as the diagnostic. Only outcome=ok maps to result=measured (with no escalate). Grammar accepts outcome=failed alongside result=measured and no escalate; this contract is the named enforcement hook that rejects that rubber-stamp.
dry_run_result.description: Outcome of the migration dry-run. M3 Measured|Omitted on `status`: 'measured' = dry-run executed (value carries outcome + the command that ran); 'omitted' = dry-run tool absent/failed/timeout (R22). x-validator-contract (M2): re-execute the dry-run, re-hash its stdout, assert the recorded outcome; AND enforce outcome∈{failed,partial} ⇒ escalate(failure_origin=test) present and result≠measured (see contract above).

outcome.description: Dry-run verdict: did the migration apply cleanly and reverse cleanly? 'ok' = dry-run passed → result=measured; 'failed'|'partial' = the migration script itself is broken (Test's in-scope failure) → NOT mapped to a result; escalate(failure_origin=test) with dry_run_detail.

dry_run_detail.description: Diagnostic text for a failed|partial dry-run (accompanies the escalate). Omitted on outcome=ok.

command.description: Exact dry-run command executed (e.g. 'bun run migrate --dry-run').

raw_stdout_sha256.description: REQUIRED SHA-256 of the dry-run stdout — the load-bearing anchor the M2 contract re-hashes to re-derive the recorded outcome. Required on the Measured branch so the outcome the M2 re-hash re-derives always has a hash anchor (mirrors red_confirmation).

reversible.description: Whether the dry-run confirmed the migration is reversible / rollback-safe.

firebat_scan.$comment: agents/test.md L22 'Run firebat scan after writing test code'. firebat is MCP-backed → degradable (Measured|Omitted, R22/M3). CONDITIONALLY REQUIRED (root allOf): whenever a primary behavior-test block (tests_added/coverage_added) is non-empty this field is REQUIRED — the mandatory scan's non-execution must surface as the Omitted branch, never as field-omission. This trigger is NOT guarded by escalate-absence (Defect-1 fix): the cap_exceeded escalate path writes a PARTIAL tests_added (real, executed test code), so the scan is required there too. Precondition/runner-absent escalates and measured(Performance|Migration)/no_op flows write no test code, so it is absent there.
firebat_scan.description: Result of the firebat scan run after writing test code (agent rule). CONDITIONALLY REQUIRED whenever the step wrote a non-empty primary behavior-test block (tests_added/coverage_added) — the scan is mandatory on test-writing flows, so its non-execution is the Omitted branch, not field-omission. This holds even on the cap_exceeded escalate path, which writes+executes a partial tests_added (real test code that must also be scanned). firebat is MCP-backed and degradable: M3 Measured|Omitted on `status` — 'measured' = scan ran (value carries findings count as an R23 CountClaim); 'omitted' = firebat unavailable/failed/timeout (R22 first-class absence). NOT nullable, NOT a placeholder.

findings_count.description: R23 CountClaim — number of firebat findings (measured count, not a bare integer).

based_on.$comment: §5 — cross-step refs are Postgres RowRefs, never file paths. Test requires its driving input ref (spec_ref XOR decide_ref, mirrored in `input`) and may carry optional enrichment refs (investigate_ref for edge-case priority, ground_ref for volatile baseline). NOTE: the exactly-one-of (spec_ref XOR decide_ref) is grammar-enforced on the `input` discriminant (input_kind oneOf), NOT here — the shared BasedOn $def is intentionally all-optional (it also carries enrichment refs); M2 cross-checks that based_on's driving ref mirrors input.input_kind. The loose BasedOn $ref is therefore deliberate, not drift.
based_on.description: §5 BASED-ON bundle. RowRefs (Postgres step_runs rows) for the upstream steps Test consumed — NEVER file paths. The driving input (spec_ref or decide_ref) mirrors the `input` discriminant. Optional enrichment: investigate_ref (risk_surface for edge-case priority), ground_ref (volatile_state pass/fail baseline). x-validator-contract (M2): the present spec_ref/decide_ref matches `input.input_kind` and the orchestrator's chain.

unverified.$comment: R13 floor at step level, KEEP polarity. Aggregate flag that any tests_added[].unverified is true (or a step-level training-data assertion is unverified). NO 'no unverified remain' forbid — Verify is the single gate.
unverified.description: R13 KEEP-polarity floor flag (step level). true = this Test output carries at least one unverified claim that MUST propagate to Verify. DESIGNED to persist (enforcement.md L41 authoritative); never forbidden true here. The historical (now-removed) 'No [UNVERIFIED] remain' self-validation rule is DELETED — Verify is the single gate that resolves unverified claims.

declared_next_step.$comment: R16 — Test may DECLARE its successor (normally 'implement', the GREEN partner), but the orchestrator INJECTS the authoritative expected_next_step. Advisory only; chain check is M2 (folded into R27).
declared_next_step.description: R16 DECLARED-NEXT-STEP (advisory). Test names its expected successor (normally Implement, the GREEN partner in the RED-GREEN loop). This does NOT free-fill the authoritative value — the orchestrator injects expected_next_step. x-validator-contract (M2): assert declared_next_step == orchestrator's expected_next_step (chain divergence catch, R16 folded into R27).
